message:
perf: use insert_from_slice for zero-copy header insertion

Optimize HTTP header parsing by using the new insert_from_slice() method
instead of the previous insert(header.name().to_string(), header.value().to_vec())
pattern that caused double allocation.

The key improvement:
- Previously: to_string() always allocated, even for lowercase header names
- Now: insert_from_slice() only allocates when uppercase bytes are detected,
  avoiding unnecessary allocation for the common case of already-lowercase
  HTTP header names (which most clients send)

Applied to both Parser and StatefulParser header insertion loops at lines
767 and 961 respectively.

This complements the insert_from_slice() and insert_lowercase() methods
added to fastapi-core's request.rs that perform conditional lowercase
conversion only when uppercase ASCII bytes are present.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
